author: @DoubleEagle76
text:
I was a Scout in my youth, reaching the rank of Life Scout.  One short of Eagle.  When my son joined Scouting, I joined as an adult leader and served 15 years in various adult leadership areas.  My son made Eagle, and we filled a small church sanctuary with family and friends, much like a wedding reception.  Our local Congressman was in attendance.  He brought a US flag flown over the Capitol as a gift.

Fast forward to 2012 when Scouting went woke.  My son pulled my grandsons out of scouting and no longer even mentions his Eagle status.  I dropped off of the local Scouting Board.  We went from strong Scouting supporters to a total boycott of the organization.  I know several of those now adult Eagle Scouts.  To a man, they've abandoned Scouting.  That Citizenship in Society was REQUIRED for Eagle and included activism in DEI causes!  Imagine, a boy is prevented from attaining the Eagle rank because he refuses to be an active participant in DEI!!!

Sorry for the TL:DR, you may sense my deep sense of anger and frustration.  I hope Hegseth knocked some sense into them, but I'm too disgusted to go back.

author: @mattstorey7256
text:
You were wondering why the boy scouts were so desperate for money? *Raise hand* I know. Here comes the story, long as it may be.


The two biggest backers of boy scout pulled out of BSA. When for years the LGBTQ were always agitating for "gay" scouts and later "gay" scoutmasters be recognized and allowed into scouts. BSA resisted and were constantly fending off law suits and harassment. BSAs position for years was they were a quasi religious organization, that sexual orientation was not part of curriculum. In essence "don't ask, don't tell" was their position. If you happen to be gay and want to be a scout, we don't care, join. BUT, if you want to make it a issue, I'm a "GAY SCOUT" not a boy scout, then there is a problem. They didn't want to be part of a political agenda. And, scouts that happened to be gay joined. But later, once they got their toe in the door, they wanted openly gay scouts masters. BSA said, "Not on your life." There aren't going to be gay troops with a gay scout master...again we don't want to be part of your agenda, take it elsewhere. But the "boys" (really activists) started agitating that they needed their own leaders, their own representation they were "comfortable" with, who would know their special challenges.

Then the lawsuits. San Diego and San Francisco used their city laws, saying non-inclusionary organizations couldn't do business with them (lots of other municipalities too, I justly know them specifically).

All the lawsuits, all the hassle. Government forces threatened their tax exempt status. Special interests were bleeding them to death by a thousand cuts...IE lawfare.

So they caved. They agreed to special gay only troops, led by gay scout masters.

AND THEN THE REAL NIGHTMARE.

85+% troops in existence were religious backed troops. The two biggest backers? LDS (mormons) and Southern Baptists. The Mormons accounted alone for more than 60%  of BSAs operating capital. They had a issue with the sexualization for scouting. So they told BSA that their new stance was a regrettable decision, they understood why, but they would not be a part of it. They gave them a year to reconsider, come up with another solution or Mormons were pulling out of the BSA, a month later, the Baptists followed suit. Several other religious based organizations also did as well.

With such a crisis on the horizon, a known need for capital, that's when they doubled down and allowed girls into BSA. A new demographic. To stop the law suits and acknowledge females being admitted, they changed the name.

True to their word, once the year was up, a month or so later the Mormons and Baptists parted ways with the scouts and started their own "children of youth" programs.

Letting the girls in did not solve their fiscal woes. Now who hassled them? The Girls Scouts. They needed their little cookie peddling money makers back. Who is going to make us rich selling corporate cookies at a tax exempt status?! Give us our girls back. The girls however...didn't want to go back. They didn't want to peddle cookies or make macaroni art all day. They like canoeing and camping and telling ghost stories around the camp fire. Fishing might be icky...but getting to go where their brothers got to go..summer camp? They would bait the hook and deal with it.

But they didn't bring cookie revenue with them...they didn't bring church money with them and most important? The structural support. Religious parents get involved. They volunteer. They work as camp councilors, do fund raisers. Donate things from the businesses or get their employers to take a tax right off and donate.

So they still have financial problem. Have had to declare bankruptcy (another reason they changed their name). And where did they wind up in the end? Go woke...go broke. DEI? You DIE.

Now that the religions quit them though, I doubt they can get them back..certainly not the Mormons and very unlikely the Baptists. They aren't wishy washy about this type of garbage. You did it once, you will again.

author: @seanningen6016
text:
A.  As a father of a son and a daughter, I saw first hand the way Girl Scouts was organized and run often failed to give girls a program that they wanted.
B.  There was a wide spread phenomenon where dissatisfied girls just tagged along with whatever their brothers were doing in Boy Scouts, but couldn’t get anything for it.
C.  Scouts USA began just after my son aged out of the BSA.  I was NOT in support.  But they always maintained separate troops (frequently “sister” troops to existing boys’ troops.)  The Boy Scouts is supposed to teach leadership, and the boys are supposed to plan things on their own with the minimum necessary adult oversight and support.  It was an explicit rule that even if boys’ trip and girls’ troop were sharing resources, had parents supporting both troops, etc, each troop had to maintain separate children’s groups for planning and leadership.
D.  Mothers are often involved in the Boy Scouts, and there are emphatic rules separating male/female adult/child for bathroom, sleeping, any other privacy.  That only got more stringent when the girls were invited in.

author: @russpaulus9171
text:
The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints used to be a huge sponsor of scouting.

It was actually a part of the young men’s program of the church in the US and my ward in particular probably had 60 to 80 Eagle Scouts over the last 30 years. 

Then Rex Tillerson came along and wanted to admit girls into scouting. 

He scheduled a vote during our church’s summer recess. 
We asked him to defer the vote for two weeks. 
He refused.  

We were by far the largest sponsor of units and scouts at the time. 
Roughly 1/3 of the units. 

We saw the writing on the wall and scheduled an orderly transition to minimize the financial impact on BSA. 

It may have happened anyway because it aligned the US program with the rest of the worldwide church but Rex certainly accelerated the process. 

He later showed his true colors in the Trump administration.

author: @StevenRaymond-c9t
text: As an Eagle Scout who got out just before BSA went crazy, the reason it happened was because they allowed gays in Scouting. LOTS of families left after that, and a lot more stuck around just long enough to let their boys finish. They took a massive financial hit after that, so they changed their names to Scouts of Boy Scouts of America because they allowed women in. Shortly after, the Mormons left and Scouts went from hurting badly to full-on death throws. It is a disgrace to see what they have become, and I have spoken to many fellow Eagle Scouts who feel like the pride and accomplishment they had in being an Eagle Scout means little beyond their own personal honor anymore, and their honor is tarnished by the poor reputation BSA gained since they left. The last words of The Scout Oath are "morally straight," and that means nothing now since they have embraced a Godless liberal ideology. Scouts was a Christian organization, even if not all scouts were Christian, and now it is a pathetic and disgraceful inversion of everything that made it great. Even this new change is not enough. It is a step in the right direction, but I'm not sure that BSA can be saved, and I'm not sure it can be replaced with the new conservative version, Trail Life. BSA was a one-time thing that lasted a hundred years and it was thoughtlessly murdered by the treacherous leaders of the last twenty years. If it can come back, it has a long road, and it takes more than just getting rid of a DEI merit badge. They need to get rid of the girls, get rid of the gays, and embrace their moral Christian roots and their focus on leadership, outdoorsmanship, service, and masculinity. I don't know if they have the money or the stomach to do that.
